key_departures.impact: Led the team with 85 catches, 1,013 yards, and 4 TDs in his lone season. Released after his 2026 cap hit was set to balloon to $26.5M. Carries $9.7M dead cap hit. Leaves a significant void at WR1.

Started all 21 games for the Patriots in 2025. Traded to Bears for a 2027 fifth-round pick. Opens door for Jared Wilson to shift from LG to C, but creates a left guard hole. Net effect is a positional shuffle that elevates IOL need.

Career year in 2025 — blossomed as a pass-rusher and run-stuffer (disruptive space-eater). Signed 3yr/$21M with $14M guaranteed in KC. Cory Durden now slots into starting role; loss of Tonga's versatility and special-teams value is meaningful.

Breakout 2025 season: 7.5 sacks (per some reports 10.5 sacks across playoffs), 18 QB hits, 2 forced fumbles, 64.9% snap share. Addition of Dre'Mont Jones signals Chaisson is unlikely to return. Significant hole created if he departs.
Standout 2025 season: 4 interceptions (tied team lead), 6 PBUs, 91.3% defensive snap share. If he leaves, safety becomes one of the team's most glaring needs.
Signed 1yr/$3.25M with Atlanta. Veteran backup TE depth piece. Leaves Patriots without a proven No. 2 TE behind Hunter Henry.

Signed 2yr/$12M max with $5.75M guaranteed in SF. Lowe improved each of his three seasons in NE, going from overmatched backup to quality spot starter. Leaves Patriots without a proven swing tackle behind Will Campbell.
Released before free agency to save $3.5M cap. Appeared in 12 games in 2025, 21 solo tackles, 1 forced fumble. Core special teamer and rotational LB. Depth loss; team moving younger at the position.
Released in February after tearing his ACL in Week 5 of 2025. Explosive kick returner and rotational back. Leaves a hole in the return game and at RB depth.
Patriots chose not to tender the RFA, making him an unrestricted free agent. Depth CB, not a starter. Minimal impact on CB need picture.
Patriots declined to tender the RFA. Durant was on IR all of 2025. No real roster impact.

contract.notes: Base value $68M per Albert Breer; max value up to $80M per Adam Schefter/ESPN. Guaranteed money not yet fully disclosed. AAV of $17M per Spotrac (tied 23rd among WRs).

signings.analysis: Doubs (55 catches, 724 yards, 6 TDs in 2025) is a 4-year investment and the clear WR1 replacement for Diggs, stepping into a starting role immediately opposite Maye. The $17M AAV is solidly mid-tier starter money for the position. However, he was never the No. 1 receiver in Green Bay and has never topped 724 yards in a season. Patriots still lack a true X/WR1 threat — Doubs profiles as a high-end WR2 with slot versatility. 4-year length with real guarantees (exact figure TBD) signals commitment. Partially addresses the WR need; team may still pursue a WR1 via trade (A.J. Brown chatter ongoing) or the draft.

contract.notes: 3yr/$42M per Adam Schefter/ESPN; max value up to $48M. $21M guaranteed per NFL Network/Ian Rapoport.

signings.analysis: AVT projects as the starting LG, slotting between Will Campbell (LT) and Jared Wilson (who shifts from LG to C following the Bradbury trade). When healthy, he graded 10th among all OGs per PFF in 2024 with the 4th-lowest pressure rate allowed (2.8%). The 3-year deal with $21M guaranteed signals a real investment. However, AVT has had season-ending injuries in 3 of his 5 NFL seasons — torn triceps in 2022 and 2025, torn Achilles in 2023. This is a high-risk, high-upside signing. The IOL need is partially addressed (LG slot filled IF healthy), but the health risk and lack of a proven center solution behind Wilson means the IOL unit still warrants attention in the draft.

contract.notes: Per Spotrac and Aaron Wilson: 3yr/$36.5M, $23.1M guaranteed, $11M signing bonus. Salaries fully guaranteed in years 1 and 2. Max value with incentives ~$39.5M.

signings.analysis: Jones (career-high 7 sacks, 51 pressures in 2025 split between TEN and BAL) is the 34th-highest paid EDGE at $12.17M AAV — well below the mid-tier starter threshold of $15M+. The structure guarantees him through 2 seasons (mirroring the Harold Landry deal template). He provides a veteran presence and versatility (can kick inside to 3-tech). However, he's not a star-caliber pass rusher and fills a rotational/second EDGE role more than a true No. 1 replacement for Chaisson. With Chaisson's departure still likely, the team still desperately needs EDGE reinforcement. Jones is a floor-setter, not a ceiling-raiser. EDGE remains a top draft need.

contract.notes: 3yr/up to $12M, $6M fully guaranteed per NFL Network's Mike Garafolo. Spotrac lists 3yr/$10.8M base.

signings.analysis: Gilliam is the best fullback in football — opened holes for the NFL's leading rusher James Cook in Buffalo. He played 21% of offensive snaps and 77% on special teams in 2025. Josh McDaniels has always employed a fullback-centric offense and the Patriots badly lacked a true FB after Brock Lampe's injury in 2025 camp. 3-year deal shows long-term commitment. This is a need fully addressed at a specialty position.

signings.previous_team: Arizona Cardinals (via LA Rams practice squad)

contract.notes: Veteran minimum deal. $1.145M fully guaranteed per Aaron Wilson/Pats Pulpit.

signings.analysis: Veteran minimum deal for a core special-teamer and depth EDGE. Luketa had 3 sacks in 10 games with Arizona in 2024, then was on the Rams' practice squad in 2025. He carves out a role primarily on special teams. This is a camp body / depth signing — does not move the needle on the EDGE need at all.

contract.notes: 2yr/$4.4M base, max $7.4M per Spotrac/ESPN Adam Schefter. $2M guaranteed.

signings.analysis: Re-signed third-string QB (did not take a snap in 2025). Retains depth behind Drake Maye and Joshua Dobbs. QB was never a need — this is roster maintenance. QB room remains unchanged. No impact on draft needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Doubs (4yr/$68M base) is a real 4-year commitment and fills the WR1 void left by Diggs. He's a good slot/possession receiver with strong hands and playoff experience (20 catches, 371 yards, 2 TDs in 4 postseason games). However, he has never topped 724 yards in a season and was never the No. 1 option in Green Bay. Patriots still lack a true alpha WR for Drake Maye. A.J. Brown trade chatter is live. Need drops from urgent Tier 1 to Tier 2 — a high-end WR or true X receiver via the draft or trade remains important but is no longer the single most pressing need.

Bradbury trade (LG → LT bump for Jared Wilson to C) created a new LG hole that AVT fills — IF healthy. The AVT deal at $14M AAV/3 years is a genuine starter investment. But AVT's 3 season-ending injuries in 5 years introduces massive health risk, and the center position (Wilson is unproven as starter) is also a question mark. IOL need holds at Tier 2: the team should still target an IOL in the draft, ideally a day 2 center/guard with starter upside.

Jones signing ($12.17M AAV, below mid-tier EDGE money) is a rotational piece at best. Chaisson is very likely gone (his breakout 7.5-sack season priced him into the $15-20M+ market). Harold Landry is under contract but was hampered by a knee injury in 2025. The Patriots finished tied for last in the NFL in sacks (35) in 2025. Jones + Luketa (minimum deal) does not solve the EDGE problem. EDGE remains the Patriots' single most critical draft need — this should be a Round 1 or early Round 2 target. Tier holds at 1.

Jaylinn Hawkins (4 INTs, 91.3% snap share, first-team defensive contributor) remains unsigned as of March 11. If he leaves — which is a real possibility given teams are pursuing him — the starting safety spot opposite Craig Woodson becomes a massive hole. This need has escalated from moderate to critical. Must be addressed in FA or rounds 2-3 of the draft.

Austin Hooper departed to Atlanta (1yr/$3.25M). Hunter Henry remains as the starting TE, but is entering the final year of his deal. The Patriots missed out on Isaiah Likely (signed NYG 3yr/$40M) and Cade Otton (re-signed TBD). No replacement TE has been added. The need holds at Tier 2 — a TE who can develop as a future starter or complement Henry is needed in rounds 2-4.

Tonga (3yr/$21M/$14M gtd with Chiefs) was the Patriots' primary nose tackle and run-stopper in 2025. Cory Durden takes over the starting role with Eric Gregory as backup. The run-stopping IDL depth is thin. No replacement DT has been signed in free agency. Tier holds at 2 — a developmental IDL in mid-rounds of the draft would serve this team well.

Vederian Lowe's departure (2yr/$12M to 49ers) leaves the Patriots without a proven swing tackle. Will Campbell is the starting LT, Morgan Moses the starting RT. But behind them, Marcus Bryant is the only in-house option. The team noted explicitly they'll need a veteran backup swing tackle. Need escalates from Tier 3 to Tier 2 — a veteran OT signing or mid-round draft pick is warranted.

Gibson was released after his ACL tear. The team needs a kick returner and RB2 behind Rhamondre Stevenson. No signing yet, but this is a Day 3 draft/UDFA priority, not a top need. Tier unchanged at 3.

key_additions.headline: Lead WR replacement for Diggs; 4-year commitment gives Drake Maye a reliable starter-caliber target
Former first-round guard fills LG hole created by Bradbury trade — elite talent when healthy but major injury risk
Career-high 7 sacks in 2025 adds veteran EDGE depth, but doesn't replace Chaisson's starter production
Best fullback in football fills a critical role in Josh McDaniels' run-heavy system
Third-string QB retained; QB depth chart unchanged with Maye/Dobbs/DeVito
Veteran minimum depth EDGE and core special-teamer; camp competition body

key_losses.headline: Led team in 2025 with 85 catches/1,013 yards; released due to ballooning $26.5M cap hit and off-field concerns
Starting center who started all 21 games traded away, triggering domino shuffle of interior OL
Career-year NT and run-stopper departs; significant loss of versatility and pass-rush ability at IDL
Breakout 2025 star (7.5+ sacks) is a pending UFA; departure essentially certain after Jones signing
Standout 2025 starter (4 INTs, 91.3% snap share) remains unsigned; safety becomes critical need if he leaves
Veteran backup TE departs; leaves Patriots thin at TE2 behind Hunter Henry
Quality swing tackle departed; leaves Patriots without a proven backup behind Campbell and Moses

notes: Free agency officially opens March 11 at 4 PM ET. All signings above were reported during the legal tampering window (March 9-10) by multiple credible sources (Rapoport, Schefter, Garafolo, Pelissero, Breer). Contract figures sourced from Spotrac, OverTheCap, and verified reporter disclosures. Romeo Doubs' exact guaranteed money not yet publicly confirmed; base value $68M/max $80M is credible per Albert Breer and Adam Schefter. K'Lavon Chaisson and Jaylinn Hawkins remain unsigned UFAs as of this report — their fates will significantly alter the EDGE and S need tiers. The Bradbury trade involved pick compensation (2027 5th to NE), reducing future draft capital slightly.
